title: eriogonum racemosum var. orthocladon
intro: Eriogonum racemosum var. orthocladon, also known as the alpine buckwheat or buckwheat sage, is a perennial plant that belongs to the buckwheat family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, and is said to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of alpine buckwheat include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Alpine buckwheat can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, with some practitioners using it topically to accelerate wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
